HomePage.title: Welcome to Mulenge Scholars Network Canada
HomePage.description: This is the home page. Navigate to the {privacyLink}.

PrivacyPage.title: Privacy Policy
PrivacyPage.description: Your privacy is important to us. This privacy statement explains the personal data we process, how we process it, and for what purposes.

information.title: Information We Collect
body: We collect information to provide better services to all our users.

howWeUse.title: How We Use Information
body: We use the information we collect from all of our services to provide, maintain, protect and improve them.

contactSection.title: Have questions?
contactSection.body: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at {emailLink}.

01.title: Workshops & Community Engagement
01.intro: We organize interactive workshops both virtual and in-person focused on:
01.footer: These sessions create safe and engaging spaces where youth can learn, connect, and grow.

02.title: High School Support (Grades 11-12)
02.intro: We provide targeted support to help students transition successfully into post-secondary education:
bullets: Access to leadership and volunteer opportunities

03.title: Adult Learning & Career Pathways
03.intro: We support adult learners who want to upgrade their education or explore new career opportunities:
bullets: Support with enrolling in adult education programs
Information on skilled trades and alternative career paths

04.title: Rebuilding Futures Initiative
04.intro: We are committed to expanding our impact globally by supporting Banyamulenge youth living in refugee camps, particularly in Kenya, Uganda, and Burundi. This initiative focuses on vocational training in high demand fields such as:
04.footer: Our goal is to equip youth with practical, employable skills that promote self-reliance and long-term stability.

TheChallenge.heading: Navigating New Systems
TheChallenge.subheading: For Banyamulenge families, displacement results in fragmented academic journeys. Transitioning into Canada is a multi-generational hurdle.
TheChallenge.criticalAwarenessBody: Financial pressures often push our youth toward short-term labor instead of long-term professional careers. MSNC exists to rewrite this trajectory.
barriers: Lack of guidance on academic pathways

mentorship.title: Mentorship
mentorship.detail: Direct mentorship from experienced students and professionals who understand the unique challenges of navigating the Canadian system.

academic.title: Academic Guidance
academic.detail: Course selection and tutoring for Grades 11–12, ensuring a smooth transition into high-level post-secondary education.

postSec.title: Post-Secondary
postSec.detail: Full support for college and university planning, applications, and accessing leadership or volunteer opportunities.

career.title: Career Pathways
career.detail: Guidance on skilled trades, adult education programs, and personalized career planning for adult learners.

global.title: Global Impact
global.detail: Supporting youth in refugee camps across Kenya, Uganda, and Burundi with training in Construction, IT, and Mechanics.

GlobalImpact.heading: Our Impact
GlobalImpact.visionStatement: We envision a future where Banyamulenge youth flourish as self-reliant community leaders.
GlobalImpact.strategicInterconnect: Strategic Interconnect: Vision points aligned to Core Pillars

01.label: Foundation
01.title: Rooted in identity, thriving in Canada
01.body: Youth who navigate two worlds with confidence, grounded in Banyamulenge heritage while fully engaged in Canadian society.

02.label: Education
02.title: From Grade 11 to university graduation
02.body: Every scholar supported through the academic pipeline, from high school transitions to post-secondary completion and beyond.

03.label: Professionalism
03.title: Established in meaningful and sustainable careers
03.body: Graduates building purposeful careers in skilled trades, professional fields, and leadership roles.

04.label: Stewardship
04.title: Leaders who give back to their communities
04.body: A generation of Banyamulenge leaders who uplift others, mentoring the next cohort and expanding opportunity.

Testimonials.heading: Real journeys.
Testimonials.defaultQuote: Empowerment through education is the bridge to our future.

noEvents.heading: No Upcoming Events
noEvents.body: We're planning our next chapter. Join our newsletter to stay updated.

donate.label: Support
donate.title: Fund a Scholar
donate.body: Your contribution directly funds mentorship programs, educational resources, and scholarships for Banyamulenge youth.

volunteer.label: Volunteer
volunteer.title: Share Your Skills
volunteer.body: Mentor a student, lead a workshop, or offer career guidance. Your experience can change a trajectory.

partner.label: Partner
partner.title: Institutional Partnership
partner.body: Align your organization with MSNC's mission. Together we build systemic pathways for the next generation.

join.label: Network
join.title: Join the Network
join.body: Are you a Banyamulenge student or graduate? Join our growing community of scholars across Canada.

Contact.heading: Connect with
Contact.subheading: Direct communication channels for scholars, partners, and institutional stakeholders.
